question.text: A merchant banker managing an open offer is required to submit a compliance report to SEBI. When must this report be submitted?

answer.text: Within 5 working days of the public announcement
After completion of all open offer obligations
On a quarterly basis during the offer period

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The merchant banker must submit a compliance report to SEBI after the completion of all open offer obligations. This report confirms that the offer was conducted in accordance with SAST Regulations, including payment to shareholders and settlement. **The Trap:** "Before the tendering period" relates to filing the letter of offer, not the compliance report. The compliance report is a post-completion obligation. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) tests the full lifecycle of MB obligations from pre-offer due diligence to post-completion compliance reporting. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The compliance report is the merchant banker's sign-off on the entire process — ensure all documentation is in order before filing, as SEBI may scrutinize any discrepancies.
